commit.message:
fix: update Resend from address to hello@kulaire.com

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

parity: des.terrain() routes through sel_set_ter for HWALL horizontal

des.terrain() set loc.typ directly, bypassing sel_set_ter()'s logic for
setting horizontal=1 on HWALL/IRONBARS tiles. Now all des.terrain() paths
call sel_set_ter(clear:false) matching C's lspo_terrain() → sel_set_ter().

Fixes seed331 mapdump H-section (Pillars themeroom). Mapdump: 3→2 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: runtime safety fixes for scanner and watcher (#394)

* fix: resolve high-severity runtime safety issues (#351)

- Fix watcher pollDirectories race condition: collect all map state
  under a single lock, perform filesystem I/O outside the lock, then
  update state under the lock. Prevents read-check-act races when
  refreshPollPaths modifies the map concurrently.

- Fix watcher refreshWatchPaths deadlock risk: move blocking fsnotify
  Add/Remove calls and readDirSnapshot outside the mutex. The lock is
  only held to compute add/remove sets and to update internal state.

- Fix scanner goroutine leak: replace context.WithoutCancel (which
  creates immortal goroutines) with a shutdown-aware context. Add
  Shutdown() method to scanner.Service, called during app shutdown.

- Fix HTTP response body not drained on non-200: add io.Copy to
  io.Discard before returning errors in image/processor.go and
  duckduckgo/duckduckgo.go to allow HTTP connection reuse.

- Fix webhook dispatcher semaphore leak on panic: add recover() in
  the delivery goroutine so panics are logged and the semaphore is
  released instead of crashing the process.

Findings #6 (event bus silent drops) and #7 (router inconsistent
locking) were verified as already addressed in the current code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #389 review comments

- Scanner: add sync.WaitGroup so Shutdown() waits for goroutines
- Watcher: only log "stopped watching" when Remove() succeeds
- Watcher: remove unused interval field from pollEntry struct
- Webhook: add debug.Stack() to panic recovery log

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address second round of PR #389 review comments

- watcher: only delete paths from watching/knownDirs maps when
  watcher.Remove() succeeds, preventing state inconsistency
- main: reverse shutdown order so HTTP server drains before scanner
  Shutdown() is called, preventing WaitGroup Add/Wait race
- scanner: add TestShutdownCancelsInProgressScan to verify the
  shutdown lifecycle (cancellation + WaitGroup wait)
- image: use _, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, ...) instead of
  //nolint:errcheck for consistency with rest of codebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: portal en SupplementManager para evitar salto en móvil

El bottom sheet se renderizaba dentro del árbol del Dashboard
(.page.stagger), cuya animación CSS crea un nuevo contexto de
apilamiento que rompe position:fixed en Safari/iOS — causando
que el sheet apareciera relativo al contenedor en vez del viewport.

Solución: createPortal(content, document.body) para que el overlay
y el sheet sean hijos directos del body, completamente fuera del
árbol animado.

chore(rules): rules diet + model routing doc drift (CAB-1697) (#1496)

## Summary
- **CAB-1697**: Compress 6 largest glob-scoped rule files from 92KB to
28KB (70% reduction)
- Fix model routing doc drift in `cost-guardrails.md` and
`autonomous-factory.md` to match actual `model-router.sh` (Haiku ≤3pts,
Opus 4-5pts+)

`operations.log` (527→500)

| File | Before | After | Reduction |
|------|--------|-------|-----------|
| gateway-arena.md | 20.5K | 6.3K | 69% |
| instance-dispatch.md | 14.5K | 4.3K | 71% |
| autonomous-factory.md | 21.5K | 4.6K | 79% |
| gateway-adapters.md | 17K | 5.5K | 68% |
| seo-content.md | 15K | 3.3K | 78% |
| cost-guardrails.md | 3.8K | 4.0K | doc drift fix |

## Test plan
- [ ] All rule files still have valid `globs:` frontmatter
- [ ] No behavioral change — compression + doc accuracy fix only

commit.message:
fix: `swamp repo upgrade` merges instructions instead of overwriting (#630)

## Summary

Fixes #628 — `swamp repo upgrade` was fully overwriting
`CLAUDE.md`/`AGENTS.md` with the swamp template, destroying any
project-specific content users had added.

- Adopt the same section-marker pattern already used for `.gitignore`
management
- Shared instruction files (`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`) now use HTML
comment markers to delimit the swamp-managed section
- Tool-specific files (`.cursor/rules/swamp.mdc`,
`.kiro/steering/swamp-rules.md`) continue to be fully overwritten since
they are not shared with user content
- On `init`: if a `CLAUDE.md` already exists, the managed section is
merged in rather than skipping the file
- On `upgrade`, four cases are handled: no file, file with markers, file
with legacy content, file with no swamp content

## Test plan

- [x] `deno check` — passes
- [x] `deno lint` — passes  
- [x] `deno fmt` — passes
- [x] `deno run test` — 2700/2700 passed
- [x] `deno run compile` — binary compiles
- [ ] Manual: `swamp repo init` in empty dir creates CLAUDE.md with
markers
- [ ] Manual: `swamp repo init` in dir with existing CLAUDE.md merges
managed section
- [ ] Manual: Add user content to CLAUDE.md, `swamp repo upgrade`
preserves it

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Blake Irvin <blakeirvin@me.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Four-part author attribution: human · tool · model · agent

- tool: the interface used (Claude Code → anthropic.com/claude-code)
- model: the specific model (Claude Sonnet 4.6 → /model/[slug])
- agent: the behavioral context (unratified-agent → /agent/[slug])

segment links to a list of posts sharing that dimension. All 18
posts updated, schema updated, RSS string updated.

commit.message:
Fix 15 data integrity, locking & security bugs across 10 files

Critical: doc lock deadlock in contradiction resolution (update_document
called inside held lock — restructured to separate lock scopes), prompt
injection on first-turn chat message (escape_xml now unconditional),
non-atomic file writes (tempfile + os.replace).

High: MongoDB ReDoS via unescaped $regex (re.escape applied), crash
recovery document drift detection, lock document init at startup,
MongoDB mirror written before file (Render source of truth).

Medium: orphaned session cleanup on batch_commit failure, rollback
reordered (file write before MongoDB deletes), silent recovery failure
now logged, quick note checks insert return value.

Low: PBKDF2 key stretching for HMAC cookies, feedback themes failure
logged, lock error message sanitized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Accept ADR-0009 and supersede ADR-0006 (#2044)

ADR-0009 (process-per-repo) supersedes ADR-0006 (in-process
RepoRuntime isolation) for supervisor integration. ADR-0006
mandated RepoRuntime as the unit of start/stop; ADR-0009 adopts
subprocess.Popen instead. Also syncs README index statuses for
ADRs 0007, 0014, 0016, 0017, 0019 already accepted.

Closes #2016

Co-authored-by: T-rav-Hydra-Ops <t@koderex.dev>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  Pulse Score, alpha, vol, Sharpe, Sortino, and Beta use the right data
- Fix annualization: use 52 periods/yr for weekly 6M/12M data, 252 for
  daily 1M/3M/YTD (matching Yahoo Finance interval selection)
- Fix Sortino annualization to use the same periodsPerYear variable
- Fix drawdown flag thresholds: 8% (1M), 12% (3M/YTD), 18% (6M/12M)
- Remove stale build error log files

Fix session duration, timestamp parsing, and v3 duplicate visitors

- Replace deriveSession() with deriveSessions(): 30-min gap splitting,
  inter-event duration capped at 5 min, idle-aware, 10s last-event credit
- Add eventTimestamp() fallback to event_data.timestamp for Firebase

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace time.Sleep waits with proper synchronization in 10 test files

Replace 29 flaky time.Sleep calls with deterministic synchronization:
- Poll Addr()/readiness methods for listener startup (syslog, relp)
- Retry-dial loops for ingesters without Addr() (fluentfwd, otlp)
- Poll job status instead of sleep-after-done (jobtracker)
- Raft config polling instead of 500ms fixed sleep (cluster)
- Deadline-based loops instead of fixed-iteration (cluster, relp)
- runtime.Gosched() for in-process atomic polling (lifecycle)

Closes gastrolog-26rw

commit.message:
Flush preferences on beforeunload to preserve view on refresh

The debounced pushPreferences could leave stale view state in Supabase
when the page closes before the 1s timer fires. Now flushes immediately
on beforeunload so pullPreferences restores the correct view.

Replace backdrop click handler with document-level listener that checks
if the click target is outside the panel. Prevents mobile ghost-click and
touch pass-through issues with overlapping fixed-position elements that
caused the drawer to dismiss when toggling route checkboxes.

commit.message:
feat: organizer reviews with tags, photos & anonymous posting (#146) (#148)

* chore: add staging branch workflow (#139)

Add staging as an intermediate branch between feature branches and main.
PRs now target staging for batch testing before a single production deploy.

- CI triggers on pushes/PRs to both main and staging
- E2E tests run on push to both main and staging
- CLAUDE.md updated with new branch workflow docs

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add promote-staging workflow with CI + deploy guards (#141)

Adds a manually triggered workflow that verifies CI and Vercel deployment
status on staging before merging to main. Prevents promoting broken builds.

Guards:
- CI must have passed on the latest staging commit
- Vercel deployment must have succeeded (or be absent)
- Uses --ff-only merge to keep history clean
- Auto-resets staging to main after promotion

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite XSS phase as single systematic test block

Phase 5 now runs as ONE complete run_python block that:
- Part A: iterates ALL forms, probes reflection, detects context, tests payloads
- Part B: tests ALL URL params from crawl
- Part C: tests stored XSS on POST forms (comments, messages, notes, etc.)
Each part uses the actual crawled data, not hardcoded endpoints.

  da navigation context que crasheavam ao serializar props

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix %!(EXTRA <nil>): Function.apply() and Array.prototype self-reference

- MethodCall("apply"/"call"): handle Function.prototype.apply(thisArg,
  argsArray) and .call(thisArg, ...args) natively on JSValue functions.
  JS code using format.apply(null, args) for string formatting (y18n)
  was silently failing since JSValue functions had no apply method.

- ArrayPrototype.prototype self-reference: the transpiler resolves
  Array to ArrayPrototype, so Array.prototype.slice becomes
  ArrayPrototype.Get("prototype").Get("slice"). Without the self-
  reference, this returned undefined, breaking y18n's argument
  extraction (Array.prototype.slice.call(arguments)).

Together these fix the %!(EXTRA <nil>) formatting error in yargs help
output, where y18n's __/__n localization functions couldn't properly
format strings via format.apply().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(server): exempt streaming and media endpoints from rate limiting (#67)

SSE connections, audio streaming, cover art, and artist photos are
high-traffic by nature and not abuse vectors. Exempting them prevents
429 errors during normal browsing and playback. Also bumps the general
limit from 300 to 500 to accommodate SPAs making parallel API calls.
